DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS LINDA HARRINGTON AND JONATHAN ROSS TO RESPOND FURTHER TO INTERROGATORIES IS MOOT AS FURTHER RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INADEQUATE INITIAL RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $594.50, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Linda Harrington And Jonathan Ross'S Further Responses To Interrogatories And Req. For Snactions
Off Calendar Mar-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendants Linda Harrington and Jonathan Ross To Respond Further To Interrogatories is moot as further responses have been served. The court finds that the inadequate initial responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendants in the amount of $594.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
